extraction.one_line_summary: PORTE Richie defended yellow by 17 seconds after PADUN Mark won from breakaway on final HC climb to Joux-Plane; PORTE avoided pushing hard on ascent to preserve energy.
tactical_patterns: PORTE deliberately conserved energy on Joux-Plane climb rather than matching attacks, prioritizing margin defense over stage result.
PADUN doubled stage wins by targeting KOM points from breakaway, calculated to overtake CRADDOCK in polka dot competition.
LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel risked nature break with 30 miles remaining, signaling tactical naivety when breakaway gap still under three minutes.
Ineos controlled tempo immediately after PORTE mechanical to prevent rivals exploiting momentary isolation with teammates pacing back.

rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: demonstrated tactical maturity by not overextending on final climb despite pressure, reversing past descent struggles
rider_signals.PADUN Mark: positive: back-to-back HC stage wins at 24 years old reveals emerging Grand Tour climbing threat beyond one-day performances
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: crashed on Joux-Plane descent but recovered to preserve podium, hip damage suggests descending aggression despite conditions
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: poorly timed nature break with 30 miles left when race still contested shows questionable race situation awareness
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: negative: lost KOM despite 18-point advantage after failing to defend from breakaway, passive jersey defense
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: struggling at tail end of peloton throughout week confirms continued lack of climbing form
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexey: positive: second overall and stage 4 ITT winner demonstrates rare combination of time trial power and stage race climbing
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: positive: nearly won four stages showing versatility beyond pure sprinting, fastest rider across varied terrain
